title: Orchestrator
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:The Orchestrator}} {{ArmaverseVoidTheme}} {{Background|file=Orchestratorbg.png}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Mate+SC&family=Single+Day&display=swap'); @import url('https://fonts.cdnfonts.com/css/bv-rondes'); }}
'''The Orchestrator'''
At first, they will be invisible.
To remain casual is to remain blissfully oblivious of what is going on behind the scenes.
But there is something. Something always there.
'''Strings.'''
Thin lines tethering you to this hell you've become familiar with.
''But who? Why? When?''
Should you have pondered this question, you've probably been able to see them. And you'd have a million questions, answers? None.
You'd try to look up to see who is pulling, who is '''holding''', and nothing but the monotony of The False Reality would meet the naked eye.
So, let's cut to the chase.
If you're here, reading this, it's because you want to understand. Find this answer that has laid out of reach for so long ever since you stepped foot here.
Don't worry. For your answers lie here.
But before we begin, let's make an agreement.
I am but a pawn. Another vessel belonging to man, who has took his time to write stories, because that's all they could do.
Everything that I tell you, everything that I know, I chose to share it with you.
Who knows if perhaps we've even met before? Maybe physically, or through ink on paper. No matter.
When you read these... I hope you're aware the things you're learning, they are but part of another script. Another scrap in a huge machine.
For both of our sakes, don't cross your lines. Don't try to burn these strings.
For if you do, you'll be involving yourself in tribulations beyond your comprehension.
Don't take this as an ominous warning. Take it as advice between two old friends.
So, now that I've established my terms, let's seal the deal.
{{Collapse|initial=Your Signature:[[File:Sketch line2.png|center|200px|link= ]]|num=1|final=Your Signature: {{USERNAME}}[[File:Sketch line2.png|center|270px|link= ]]|body= Good. So we have an agreement.
So, let me introduce myself.
It's in my best interest to remain anonymous, so you can call me, between us friends, '''The Author.'''
Now, let's get to what you came here for.
''Who is The Orchestrator?''
Good question. Because nobody knows, truly.
What he is, how he came to be, or why he does what he does, nobody knows.
But if you asked me?
He is a '''presence'''.
You might disregard that odd feeling that is always creeping into you as hallucinations, phenomenons. Perhaps you're going insane.
But that is ''him'', undeniably there. He wants you to think the latter, because then, you'd stop questioning. Stop pondering around his carefully crafted scripts.
For should you step where you're not meant to, he'll send one of the [[IDI|many]] [[The Umbra|proxies]] he has.
But why? What is he trying to hide from you so adamantly?
Simple. Nothing.
He is not hiding anything.
This perfect, flawless plan that he has conjured is quite unsteady. For the recent disasters you've seen ravaging your realm are not his doing, no.
You see, he has been waiting. Ever since we first started setting camp in this reality, he's been waiting for the perfect time for him to take the chance to begin his schemes.
For what reason? It is complicated.
It all starts with a question.
Any wanderer in the False Reality has seen how far humanity is willing to kill itself, to exploit each other, to cause chaos and mayhem in a place that is already hellish, a place that has already subjected many through torture nobody back home has seen.
He saw this, and wondered.
''Just how deep does this carelessness run?''
We can't say if he is ill-intended, or if he wants to teach a lesson, or to see how far he can push in this script.
And that is where we meet our paradox.
We can't blame anyone. We never will. For this force is primal. Natural. Nobody can control it.
But then, how does this happen? How does somebody manage to adapt to this catastrophe?
Simple.
Wanderers have a lot of things dragging them down.
So, sometimes, they'll give away something very key in being a human.
'''Empathy.'''
Haven't you seen this pattern? Only those who cast off the worries that this realm has brought to them, no matter how ignorant they seem, ultimately, are the ones who live in bliss! Even more so than they did back on Earth.
They thrive. Even in chaos. But at what cost?
Everything has a price here. You'll always lose something. Perhaps money. Resources. A part of yourself. It is something that remains constant, no matter where you go.
So, that's the foundation.
How much will you give away just to be free of this pain?
That is exactly what The Orchestrator wants to test.
So what is all of this? His script? A set of trials? A game? A limbo?
We can't say for sure.
But keep an eye out. Every coincidence that you witness may not be what it seems. Every timed strike is now a warning.
The Orchestrator doesn't like being defied. So you best keep quiet, and you'll remain... untouched.
'''And for those whose names have ended up in his script;''' '''Beware.'''
'''For every breath;'''
'''Every step;'''
'''Every small moment you hesitate;'''
'''It might not be your own.'''
'''Even those you love could be part of his script.'''
'''So keep an eye out.'''
'''For every word in his paper, has a destiny.'''
'''Something that awaits.'''
}}
